## Constants

<a name="DateTimeFormat"></a>

```go
const (
    // DateTimeFormat is date-time format used in iCalendar (RFC 5545)
    DateTimeFormat = "20060102T150405Z"
    // LocalDateTimeFormat is a date-time format without Z prefix
    LocalDateTimeFormat = "20060102T150405"
    // DateFormat is date format used in iCalendar (RFC 5545)
    DateFormat = "20060102"
)
```

<a name="MAXYEAR"></a>MAXYEAR

```go
const (
    MAXYEAR = 9999
)
```

## Variables

<a name="M366MASK"></a>Every mask is 7 days longer to handle cross\-year weekly periods.

```go
var (
    M366MASK     []int
    M365MASK     []int
    MDAY366MASK  []int
    MDAY365MASK  []int
    NMDAY366MASK []int

    M365RANGE    = []int{0, 31, 59, 90, 120, 151, 181, 212, 243, 273, 304, 334, 365}
)
```

<a name="StrToDates"></a>

## func [StrToDates](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/str.go#L385>)

```go
func StrToDates(str string) (ts []time.Time, err error)
```

StrToDates is intended to parse RDATE and EXDATE properties supporting only VALUE=DATE\-TIME \(DATE and PERIOD are not supported\). Accepts string with format: "VALUE=DATE\-TIME;\[TZID=...\]:\{time\},\{time\},...,\{time\}" or simply "\{time\},\{time\},...\{time\}" and parses it to array of dates In case no time zone specified in str, when all dates are parsed in UTC

<a name="StrToDatesInLoc"></a>

## func [StrToDatesInLoc](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/str.go#L391>)

```go
func StrToDatesInLoc(str string, defaultLoc *time.Location) (ts []time.Time, err error)
```

StrToDatesInLoc same as StrToDates but it consideres default location to parse dates in in case no location specified with TZID parameter

<a name="StrToDtStart"></a>

## func [StrToDtStart](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/str.go#L443>)

```go
func StrToDtStart(str string, defaultLoc *time.Location) (time.Time, error)
```

StrToDtStart accepts string with format: "\(TZID=\{timezone\}:\)?\{time\}" and parses it to a date may be used to parse DTSTART rules, without the DTSTART; part.

<a name="ROption"></a>

## type [ROption](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rrule.go#L94-L111>)

ROption offers options to construct a RRule instance. For performance, it is strongly recommended providing explicit ROption.Dtstart, which defaults to \`time.Now\(\).UTC\(\).Truncate\(time.Second\)\`.

```go
type ROption struct {
    Freq       Frequency
    Dtstart    time.Time
    Interval   int
    Wkst       Weekday
    Count      int
    Until      time.Time
    Bysetpos   []int
    Bymonth    []int
    Bymonthday []int
    Byyearday  []int
    Byweekno   []int
    Byweekday  []Weekday
    Byhour     []int
    Byminute   []int
    Bysecond   []int
    Byeaster   []int
}
```

### func [StrToROption](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/str.go#L169>)

```go
func StrToROption(rfcString string) (*ROption, error)
```

StrToROption converts string to ROption.

<a name="StrToROptionInLocation"></a>

```go
func StrToROptionInLocation(rfcString string, loc *time.Location) (*ROption, error)
```

StrToROptionInLocation is same as StrToROption but in case local time is supplied as date\-time/date field \(ex. UNTIL\), it is parsed as a time in a given location \(time zone\)

<a name="ROption.String"></a>

### func \(\*ROption\) [String](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/str.go#L125>)

```go
func (option *ROption) String() string
```

String returns RRULE string with DTSTART if exists. e.g.

```
DTSTART;TZID=America/New_York:19970105T083000
RRULE:FREQ=YEARLY;INTERVAL=2;BYMONTH=1;BYDAY=SU;BYHOUR=8,9;BYMINUTE=30
```

<a name="RRule"></a>

## type [RRule](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rrule.go#L115-L137>)

RRule offers a small, complete, and very fast, implementation of the recurrence rules documented in the iCalendar RFC, including support for caching of results.

```go
type RRule struct {
    OrigOptions ROption
    Options     ROption
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "fmt"
 "time"

 "github.com/teambition/rrule-go"
)

func printTimeSlice(ts []time.Time) {
 for _, t := range ts {
  fmt.Println(t)
 }
}

func main() {
 // Daily, for 10 occurrences.
 r, _ := rrule.NewRRule(rrule.ROption{
  Freq:    rrule.DAILY,
  Count:   10,
  Dtstart: time.Date(1997, 9, 2, 9, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
 })

 fmt.Println(r.String())
 // DTSTART:19970902T090000Z
 // RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=10

 printTimeSlice(r.All())
 // 1997-09-02 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // 1997-09-03 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // ...
 // 1997-09-07 09:00:00 +0000 UTC

 printTimeSlice(r.Between(
  time.Date(1997, 9, 6, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
  time.Date(1997, 9, 8, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC), true))
 // [1997-09-06 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 //  1997-09-07 09:00:00 +0000 UTC]

 // Every four years, the first Tuesday after a Monday in November, 3 occurrences (U.S. Presidential Election day).
 r, _ = rrule.NewRRule(rrule.ROption{
  Freq:       rrule.YEARLY,
  Interval:   4,
  Count:      3,
  Bymonth:    []int{11},
  Byweekday:  []rrule.Weekday{rrule.TU},
  Bymonthday: []int{2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8},
  Dtstart:    time.Date(1996, 11, 5, 9, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
 })

 fmt.Println(r.String())
 // DTSTART:19961105T090000Z
 // RRULE:FREQ=YEARLY;INTERVAL=4;COUNT=3;BYMONTH=11;BYMONTHDAY=2,3,4,5,6,7,8;BYDAY=TU

 printTimeSlice(r.All())
 // 1996-11-05 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // 2000-11-07 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // 2004-11-02 09:00:00 +0000 UTC

}
```

#### Output

```
DTSTART:19970902T090000Z
RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=10
1997-09-02 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-03 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-04 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-05 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-06 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-07 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-08 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-09 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-10 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-11 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-06 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-07 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
DTSTART:19961105T090000Z
RRULE:FREQ=YEARLY;INTERVAL=4;COUNT=3;BYMONTH=11;BYMONTHDAY=2,3,4,5,6,7,8;BYDAY=TU
1996-11-05 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
2000-11-07 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
2004-11-02 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
```

</p>
</details>

```go
func NewRRule(arg ROption) (*RRule, error)
```

<a name="StrToRRule"></a>

### func [StrToRRule](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/str.go#L280>)

```go
func StrToRRule(rfcString string) (*RRule, error)
```

StrToRRule converts string to RRule

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "fmt"
 "time"

 "github.com/teambition/rrule-go"
)

func printTimeSlice(ts []time.Time) {
 for _, t := range ts {
  fmt.Println(t)
 }
}

func main() {
 // Compatible with old DTSTART
 r, _ := rrule.StrToRRule("FREQ=DAILY;DTSTART=20060101T150405Z;COUNT=5")
 fmt.Println(r.OrigOptions.RRuleString())
 // FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=5

 fmt.Println(r.OrigOptions.String())
 // DTSTART:20060101T150405Z
 // RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=5

 fmt.Println(r.String())
 // DTSTART:20060101T150405Z
 // RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=5

 printTimeSlice(r.All())
 // 2006-01-01 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
 // 2006-01-02 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
 // 2006-01-03 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
 // 2006-01-04 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
 // 2006-01-05 15:04:05 +0000 UTC

}
```

#### Output

```
FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=5
DTSTART:20060101T150405Z
RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=5
DTSTART:20060101T150405Z
RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=5
2006-01-01 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
2006-01-02 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
2006-01-03 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
2006-01-04 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
2006-01-05 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="RRule.After"></a>

### func \(\*RRule\) [After](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rrule.go#L933>)

```go
func (r *RRule) After(dt time.Time, inc bool) time.Time
```

After returns the first recurrence after the given datetime instance, or time.Time's zero value if no recurrence match. The inc keyword defines what happens if dt is an occurrence. With inc == True, if dt itself is an occurrence, it will be returned. It is only supported second precision.

<a name="RRule.All"></a>

### func \(\*RRule\) [All](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rrule.go#L907>)

```go
func (r *RRule) All() []time.Time
```

All returns all occurrences of the RRule. It is only supported second precision.

<a name="RRule.Before"></a>

### func \(\*RRule\) [Before](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rrule.go#L924>)

```go
func (r *RRule) Before(dt time.Time, inc bool) time.Time
```

Before returns the last recurrence before the given datetime instance, or time.Time's zero value if no recurrence match. The inc keyword defines what happens if dt is an occurrence. With inc == True, if dt itself is an occurrence, it will be returned. It is only supported second precision.

<a name="RRule.Between"></a>

### func \(\*RRule\) [Between](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rrule.go#L915>)

```go
func (r *RRule) Between(after, before time.Time, inc bool) []time.Time
```

Between returns all the occurrences of the RRule between after and before. The inc keyword defines what happens if after and/or before are themselves occurrences. With inc == True, they will be included in the list, if they are found in the recurrence set. It is only supported second precision.

<a name="RRule.DTStart"></a>

### func \(\*RRule\) [DTStart](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rrule.go#L940>)

```go
func (r *RRule) DTStart(dt time.Time)
```

DTStart set a new DTSTART for the rule and recalculates the timeset if needed. It will be truncated to second precision. Default to \`time.Now\(\).UTC\(\).Truncate\(time.Second\)\`.

```go
func (r *RRule) GetDTStart() time.Time
```

GetDTStart gets DTSTART time for rrule

```go
func (r *RRule) GetUntil() time.Time
```

<a name="RRule.String"></a>

```go
func (r *RRule) String() string
```

<a name="RRule.Until"></a>

### func \(\*RRule\) [Until](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rrule.go#L953>)

```go
func (r *RRule) Until(ut time.Time)
```

Until set a new UNTIL for the rule and recalculates the timeset if needed. It will be truncated to second precision. Default to \`Dtstart.Add\(time.Duration\(1\<\<63 \- 1\)\)\`, approximately 290 years.

<a name="Set"></a>

## type [Set](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L12-L17>)

Set allows more complex recurrence setups, mixing multiple rules, dates, exclusion rules, and exclusion dates

```go
type Set struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "fmt"
 "time"

 "github.com/teambition/rrule-go"
)

func printTimeSlice(ts []time.Time) {
 for _, t := range ts {
  fmt.Println(t)
 }
}

func main() {
 // Daily, for 7 days, jumping Saturday and Sunday occurrences.
 set := rrule.Set{}
 r, _ := rrule.NewRRule(rrule.ROption{
  Freq:    rrule.DAILY,
  Count:   7,
  Dtstart: time.Date(1997, 9, 2, 9, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)})
 set.RRule(r)

 fmt.Println(set.String())
 // DTSTART:19970902T090000Z
 // RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=7

 printTimeSlice(set.All())
 // 1997-09-02 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // 1997-09-03 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // 1997-09-04 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // 1997-09-05 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // 1997-09-06 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // 1997-09-07 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // 1997-09-08 09:00:00 +0000 UTC

 // Weekly, for 4 weeks, plus one time on day 7, and not on day 16.
 set = rrule.Set{}
 r, _ = rrule.NewRRule(rrule.ROption{
  Freq:    rrule.WEEKLY,
  Count:   4,
  Dtstart: time.Date(1997, 9, 2, 9, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)})
 set.RRule(r)
 set.RDate(time.Date(1997, 9, 7, 9, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC))
 set.ExDate(time.Date(1997, 9, 16, 9, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC))

 fmt.Println(set.String())
 // DTSTART:19970902T090000Z
 // RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;COUNT=4
 // RDATE:19970907T090000Z
 // EXDATE:19970916T090000Z

 printTimeSlice(set.All())
 // 1997-09-02 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // 1997-09-07 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // 1997-09-09 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
 // 1997-09-23 09:00:00 +0000 UTC

}
```

#### Output

```
DTSTART:19970902T090000Z
RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=7
1997-09-02 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-03 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-04 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-05 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-06 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-07 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-08 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
DTSTART:19970902T090000Z
RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;COUNT=4
RDATE:19970907T090000Z
EXDATE:19970916T090000Z
1997-09-02 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-07 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-09 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
1997-09-23 09:00:00 +0000 UTC
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="StrSliceToRRuleSet"></a>

### func [StrSliceToRRuleSet](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/str.go#L301>)

```go
func StrSliceToRRuleSet(ss []string) (*Set, error)
```

StrSliceToRRuleSet converts given str slice to RRuleSet In case there is a time met in any rule without specified time zone, when it is parsed in UTC \(see StrSliceToRRuleSetInLoc\)

<a name="StrSliceToRRuleSetInLoc"></a>

### func [StrSliceToRRuleSetInLoc](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/str.go#L307>)

```go
func StrSliceToRRuleSetInLoc(ss []string, defaultLoc *time.Location) (*Set, error)
```

StrSliceToRRuleSetInLoc is same as StrSliceToRRuleSet, but by default parses local times in specified default location

<a name="StrToRRuleSet"></a>

### func [StrToRRuleSet](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/str.go#L289>)

```go
func StrToRRuleSet(s string) (*Set, error)
```

StrToRRuleSet converts string to RRuleSet

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "fmt"
 "time"

 "github.com/teambition/rrule-go"
)

func printTimeSlice(ts []time.Time) {
 for _, t := range ts {
  fmt.Println(t)
 }
}

func main() {
 s, _ := rrule.StrToRRuleSet("DTSTART:20060101T150405Z\nRRULE:FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=5\nEXDATE:20060102T150405Z")
 fmt.Println(s.String())
 // DTSTART:20060101T150405Z
 // RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=5
 // EXDATE:20060102T150405Z

 printTimeSlice(s.All())
 // 2006-01-01 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
 // 2006-01-03 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
 // 2006-01-04 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
 // 2006-01-05 15:04:05 +0000 UTC

}
```

#### Output

```
DTSTART:20060101T150405Z
RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=5
EXDATE:20060102T150405Z
2006-01-01 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
2006-01-03 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
2006-01-04 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
2006-01-05 15:04:05 +0000 UTC
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Set.After"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [After](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L205>)

```go
func (set *Set) After(dt time.Time, inc bool) time.Time
```

After returns the first recurrence after the given datetime instance, or time.Time's zero value if no recurrence match. The inc keyword defines what happens if dt is an occurrence. With inc == True, if dt itself is an occurrence, it will be returned. It is only supported second precision.

<a name="Set.All"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [All](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L179>)

```go
func (set *Set) All() []time.Time
```

All returns all occurrences of the rrule.Set. It is only supported second precision.

<a name="Set.Before"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [Before](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L196>)

```go
func (set *Set) Before(dt time.Time, inc bool) time.Time
```

Before Returns the last recurrence before the given datetime instance, or time.Time's zero value if no recurrence match. The inc keyword defines what happens if dt is an occurrence. With inc == True, if dt itself is an occurrence, it will be returned. It is only supported second precision.

<a name="Set.Between"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [Between](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L187>)

```go
func (set *Set) Between(after, before time.Time, inc bool) []time.Time
```

Between returns all the occurrences of the rrule between after and before. The inc keyword defines what happens if after and/or before are themselves occurrences. With inc == True, they will be included in the list, if they are found in the recurrence set. It is only supported second precision.

<a name="Set.DTStart"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [DTStart](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L44>)

```go
func (set *Set) DTStart(dtstart time.Time)
```

DTStart sets dtstart property for set. It will be truncated to second precision.

<a name="Set.ExDate"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [ExDate](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L97>)

```go
func (set *Set) ExDate(exdate time.Time)
```

ExDate include the given datetime instance in the recurrence set exclusion list. Dates included that way will not be generated, even if some inclusive rrule or rdate matches them. It will be truncated to second precision.

<a name="Set.GetDTStart"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [GetDTStart](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L53>)

```go
func (set *Set) GetDTStart() time.Time
```

GetDTStart gets DTSTART for set

<a name="Set.GetExDate"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [GetExDate](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L111>)

```go
func (set *Set) GetExDate() []time.Time
```

GetExDate returns explicitly excluded dates \(exdates\) in the set

<a name="Set.GetRDate"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [GetRDate](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L89>)

```go
func (set *Set) GetRDate() []time.Time
```

GetRDate returns explicitly added dates \(rdates\) in the set

<a name="Set.GetRRule"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [GetRRule](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L69>)

```go
func (set *Set) GetRRule() *RRule
```

GetRRule returns the rrules in the set

<a name="Set.Iterator"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [Iterator](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L134>)

```go
func (set *Set) Iterator() (next func() (time.Time, bool))
```

Iterator returns an iterator for rrule.Set

<a name="Set.RDate"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [RDate](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L75>)

```go
func (set *Set) RDate(rdate time.Time)
```

RDate include the given datetime instance in the recurrence set generation. It will be truncated to second precision.

<a name="Set.RRule"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [RRule](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L59>)

```go
func (set *Set) RRule(rrule *RRule)
```

RRule set the RRULE for set. There is the only one RRULE in the set as https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5545#appendix-A.1

<a name="Set.Recurrence"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [Recurrence](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L20>)

```go
func (set *Set) Recurrence() []string
```

Recurrence returns a slice of all the recurrence rules for a set

<a name="Set.SetExDates"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [SetExDates](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L103>)

```go
func (set *Set) SetExDates(exdates []time.Time)
```

SetExDates sets explicitly excluded dates \(exdates\) in the set. It will be truncated to second precision.

<a name="Set.SetRDates"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [SetRDates](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rruleset.go#L81>)

```go
func (set *Set) SetRDates(rdates []time.Time)
```

SetRDates sets explicitly added dates \(rdates\) in the set. It will be truncated to second precision.

<a name="Set.String"></a>

### func \(\*Set\) [String](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/str.go#L274>)

```go
func (set *Set) String() string
```

<a name="Weekday"></a>

## type [Weekday](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rrule.go#L59-L62>)

Weekday specifying the nth weekday. Field N could be positive or negative \(like MO\(\+2\) or MO\(\-3\). Not specifying N \(0\) is the same as specifying \+1.

```go
type Weekday struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Weekday.Day"></a>

### func \(\*Weekday\) [Day](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rrule.go#L77>)

```go
func (wday *Weekday) Day() int
```

Day returns index of the day in a week \(0 for MO, 6 for SU\)

<a name="Weekday.N"></a>

### func \(\*Weekday\) [N](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rrule.go#L72>)

```go
func (wday *Weekday) N() int
```

N returns index of the week, e.g. for 3MO, N\(\) will return 3

<a name="Weekday.Nth"></a>

### func \(\*Weekday\) [Nth](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/rrule.go#L67>)

```go
func (wday *Weekday) Nth(n int) Weekday
```

Nth return the nth weekday \_\_call\_\_ \- Cannot call the object directly, do it through e.g. TH.nth\(\-1\) instead,

<a name="Weekday.String"></a>

### func \(Weekday\) [String](<https://github.com/teambition/rrule-go/blob/master/str.go#L55>)

```go
func (wday Weekday) String() string
```
